questionHtml:
When a cell divides by meiosis, chiasmata can be observed. Which are features of chiasmata?

I. They are points of attachment between chromatids of non-homologous chromosomes.

II. They occur during meiosis I.

III. They increase stability of bivalents.

A. I and II only

B. I and III only

C. II and III only

D. I, II and III

meta.title: When a cell divides by meiosis, chiasmata can be observed. Which are features of chiasmata? I. They are points of attachment between chromatids of non-homologous chromosomes. II. T
